text: Python lists are ordered, mutable collections that can store elements of different data types. Lists support indexing, slicing, append, insert, remove, and sort operations.
Python dictionaries store key-value pairs. Dictionary values are accessed using keys. Common methods include get(), keys(), values(), items(), and update().
Functions in Python are defined using the def keyword. Functions can accept parameters, return values using the return statement, and improve code reusability.
A for loop in Python iterates over sequences such as lists, tuples, strings, or ranges. The range() function is commonly used to repeat actions a fixed number of times.
Exception handling in Python uses try, except, else, and finally blocks. Exceptions prevent a program from crashing when runtime errors occur.
